# UC-15.3.13 · Environmental Sensor Battery Status

## Description

Wireless environmental sensor battery health; dead batteries create monitoring gaps and risk undetected temperature or humidity exceedances.

## Value

Wireless environmental sensor battery health; dead batteries create monitoring gaps and risk undetected temperature or humidity exceedances.

## Implementation

Poll sensor management interface (SNMP, API) for battery_level_pct and last_report_time. Poll daily or every 6 hours. Alert on battery <20% or no report in 24+ hours (possible dead battery). Maintain sensor inventory with battery replacement schedule. Report on sensors due for battery change.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: SNMP modular input, custom (sensor API).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: Sensor management interface (battery_level_pct, last_report_time).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Poll sensor management interface (SNMP, API) for battery_level_pct and last_report_time. Poll daily or every 6 hours. Alert on battery <20% or no report in 24+ hours (possible dead battery). Maintain sensor inventory with battery replacement schedule. Report on sensors due for battery change.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=environment sourcetype="sensor:battery_status"
| eval hours_since_report=round((now()-last_report_time)/3600,1)
| where battery_level_pct < 20 OR hours_since_report > 24
| table sensor_id, zone, sensor_type, battery_level_pct, last_report_time, hours_since_report
| sort battery_level_pct
```

Understanding this SPL

**Environmental Sensor Battery Status** — Wireless environmental sensor battery health; dead batteries create monitoring gaps and risk undetected temperature or humidity exceedances.

Documented **Data sources**: Sensor management interface (battery_level_pct, last_report_time). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): SNMP modular input, custom (sensor API). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: environment; **sourcetype**: sensor:battery_status. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=environment, sourcetype="sensor:battery_status". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **hours_since_report** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where battery_level_pct < 20 OR hours_since_report > 24` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Pipeline stage (see **Environmental Sensor Battery Status**): table sensor_id, zone, sensor_type, battery_level_pct, last_report_time, hours_since_report
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the BMS console for the same equipment, sensors, and time range.


Step 4 — Operationalize

Step 4 — Operationalize
Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (low battery sensors), Gauge (lowest battery %), Bar chart (sensors by battery level), Single value (sensors needing battery replacement).

## SPL

```spl
index=environment sourcetype="sensor:battery_status"
| eval hours_since_report=round((now()-last_report_time)/3600,1)
| where battery_level_pct < 20 OR hours_since_report > 24
| table sensor_id, zone, sensor_type, battery_level_pct, last_report_time, hours_since_report
| sort battery_level_pct
```

## Visualization

Table (low battery sensors), Gauge (lowest battery %), Bar chart (sensors by battery level), Single value (sensors needing battery replacement).
